name: pySigma (Sigma Rule Conversion Framework)

auth.notes: Local rule conversion library; no auth

performance.notes: Rule conversion is fast; execution in target SIEM depends on rule complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Convert Sigma detection rules to SIEM query languages. Backends: Splunk (pySigma-backend-splunk), Elastic ESQL, Microsoft Sentinel KQL, QRadar, Opensearch, Azure Data Explorer. SigmaCollection.from_yaml(rule). backend.convert(collection). 10,000+ Sigma rules in SigmaHQ/sigma repository covering MITRE ATT&CK. Uncoder.io for web-based conversion. Used by SOC engineers converting community threat detection rules to target SIEM.

gotchas: Backend plugins required per SIEM platform
Some Sigma rules use features not supported by all backends
Rule quality in community varies — validate before deploying
v1 pySigma replaces older sigma-tools with breaking changes
best_when: SOC teams converting community Sigma rules to their SIEM
Automating threat detection rule deployment pipelines
Threat hunters building detections from MITRE ATT&CK techniques
avoid_when: Non-Python automation pipelines (sigma-cli may be easier)
Simple one-off rule conversion (Uncoder.io web UI simpler)
summary: pySigma converts Sigma detection rules to SIEM query languages (Splunk, KQL, Elastic, QRadar). Backend plugins for each platform. SigmaCollection.from_yaml(). Converts 10,000+ community Sigma rules from SigmaHQ/sigma. Used by SOC engineers automating detection deployment. sigma-cli for command-line; Uncoder.io for web. Essential for multi-SIEM or community-rule-consuming SOC pipelines.
